channel.title: Africa on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Africa on VitaminDWiki

item.title: HIV, TB, Malaria more important than COVID to lower Africa
item.description:
• Relative Burdens of the COVID-19, Malaria, Tuberculosis, and HIV/AIDS Epidemics in Sub-Saharan Africa
    • Note: Vitamin D may fight all 4 health problems

Relative Burdens of the COVID-19, Malaria, Tuberculosis, and HIV/AIDS Epidemics in Sub-Saharan Africa

Am. J. Trop. Med. Hyg., 105(6), 2021,pp.1510-1515 doi:10.4269/ajtmh.21-0899

David Bell1 bell00david@gmail.com and Kristian Schultz Hansen2,3

1Independent Consultant, Issaquah, Washington;

2Department of Public Health and Centre for Health Economics and Policy, University of Copenhagen, Copenhagen, Denmark;

3The National Research Center of Working Environment, Copenhagen, Denmark

item.title: Vitamin D deficiency - Ghana 43 percent
item.description:
Chart in the PDF appears to show even lower levels

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

  • African Vitamin D - 60% have less than 30 ng – Meta-analysis Oct 2019

  • Vitamin D levels of East African tribes

item.title: Vitamin D genes vary with UVB in Europe, Africa, Asia
Nigerian albinos have vitamin D levels of 96 ng
item.description:
See also web

2 million Albinos in Nigeria (population of Nigeria = 191 million)

  • An investigation into a possible relationship between vitamin D, parathyroid hormone, calcium and magnesium in a normally pigmented and an albino rural black population in the Northern Province of South Africa - 2000 DOI: 10.1002/biof.5520110110

Download the PDF from Sci-Hub via VitaminDWiki

Enechukwu N1, Cockburn M2, Ogun G3, Ezejiofor OI1, George A4, Ogunbiyi A4.

item.title: African Vitamin D - 60% have less than 30 ng – Meta-analysis
Preeclampsia 11X more likely if poor Vitamin D Binding Protein (South Africa)
